concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 1 - Data Ingestion and Quality Filtering
concerns.text: Inconsistent terminology: The User Story header refers to 'User Story 1', but the Acceptance Scenarios reference 'US-1' while the Functional Requirements section uses 'FR-001' and 'FR-002' with '(See US-1)'. The spec uses 'User Story' in headers but 'US-#' in cross-references. While understandable, this breaks strict identifier consistency. Standardize to either 'User Story 1' or 'US-1' throughout the document for cross-referencing.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Pathway Enrichment and Visualization
concerns.text: Inconsistent terminology: The User Story header refers to 'User Story 3', but the Acceptance Scenarios reference 'US-3' while the Functional Requirements section uses 'FR-005' and 'FR-006' with '(See US-3)'. The spec uses 'User Story' in headers but 'US-#' in cross-references. Standardize to either 'User Story 3' or 'US-3' throughout the document for cross-referencing.

Inconsistent terminology: The User Story header refers to 'User Story 2', but the Acceptance Scenarios reference 'US-2' while the Functional Requirements section uses 'FR-003', 'FR-004', and 'FR-007' with '(See US-2)'. The spec uses 'User Story' in headers but 'US-#' in cross-references. Standardize to either 'User Story 2' or 'US-2' throughout the document for cross-referencing.

Inconsistent terminology: The Success Criteria section uses 'SC-001' through 'SC-004' but the cross-references in the text use '(See US-1)', '(See US-2)', etc. While the US-# references are consistent with the FR section, the SC section itself does not define a consistent naming convention for the User Stories it references. Ensure all cross-references to User Stories use the same format (e.g., 'US-1' or 'User Story 1') throughout the entire document.
